description:
You are a legal research assistant preparing a reference library for submission to a law review symposium on Fourth Amendment digital privacy. The library contains 18 references (11 Supreme Court cases and 7 law review articles) imported from multiple sources with metadata errors.

Fix all metadata discrepancies — reporters, dates, courts, volumes, and page numbers — using the verified reference file at /home/ga/Documents/correct_citations.csv. Organize the library into a parent collection 'Fourth Amendment Digital Privacy' with subcollections 'Foundational Precedent' (pre-digital era Fourth Amendment cases), 'Digital Privacy Doctrine' (modern technology privacy cases), 'Scholarly Analysis' (all law review articles), and 'Limiting Authority' (cases narrowing privacy protections), assigning every item appropriately. Create Related links between these case-article pairs: Carpenter v. United States with the Freiwald article, Katz v. United States with the Kerr article, Smith v. Maryland with the Murphy article, and Riley v. California with the Tokson article. Set the Quick Copy citation style to 'JM Indigo (Law Review)' and export all items as RIS to /home/ga/Documents/symposium_bibliography.ris.

Login: username 'ga', password 'password123'.

Scholarly Analysis: The Fourth Amendment and New Technologies
Cell Phone Location Data and the Fourth Amendment
The Case Against the Case for Third-Party Doctrine

related_pairs: Cell Phone Location Data and the Fourth Amendment
The Fourth Amendment and New Technologies
The Case Against the Case for Third-Party Doctrine
